queue.task: Review today's HenryDB work: 20 bugs fixed, 200+ tests. What's the hardest remaining problem? GROUP BY alias, query optimizer gaps, or file-backed persistence?
queue.summary: Reviewed 94 tasks today. Not in breadth spiral — found genuine bugs. Hardest remaining: file-backed persistence (multi-session). This session: GROUP BY alias fix + adversarial stress testing, then Monkey evening.

queue.goal: Fix neural-net CI failures (23 legacy test failures from API mismatches, broken since Apr 11)
Deepen HenryDB: fix GROUP BY alias resolution, improve query optimizer cost model accuracy
queue.summary: Found 2 real GROUP BY bugs: (1) simple column alias not resolved (GROUP BY person gives 1 group instead of 2), (2) ordinal position not supported. Planned 3 BUILD tasks.

queue.task: Fix GROUP BY simple column alias resolution (GROUP BY person when SELECT name AS person)
queue.summary: Fixed GROUP BY simple column alias resolution. Added columnAliasMap for type:column aliases. GROUP BY person when SELECT name AS person now works correctly. All 39 GROUP BY tests pass.

queue.task: Fix GROUP BY ordinal position support (GROUP BY 1, GROUP BY 2)
queue.summary: Implemented GROUP BY ordinal position support. GROUP BY 1/2/etc now resolves to corresponding SELECT columns. Works with plain columns, expressions, functions, CASE. All 39 GROUP BY tests pass.

queue.task: Write comprehensive GROUP BY alias + ordinal test suite
queue.summary: 18 comprehensive GROUP BY alias + ordinal tests: simple/expression/CASE/function aliases, ordinal 1/2/multiple, HAVING, ORDER BY, NULL, shadowing. All pass.
Skipped — orphan placeholder from removed plan T2

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard health, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Git pushed henrydb + workspace. Dashboard healthy.

queue.task: What's the most interesting unsolved problem in HenryDB? Where would going deeper teach me the most?
queue.summary: Direction: hunt remaining test failures to push pass rate higher. Focus on whatever class of failures is most common. Alternative: query bytecode VM or incremental vacuum. Going with failure hunting — highest ROI for quality.

queue.goal: HenryDB stress testing: find bugs through adversarial query patterns and edge cases
queue.summary: Fixed histogram equality estimation bug — exact-match buckets now sum full count instead of dividing by ndv. Estimate for val=1 (900/1000 rows) went from 31 to 884. Planned 3 BUILD tasks.

queue.task: Fix histogram equality estimation for high-frequency values (DONE in PLAN)
queue.summary: Already fixed during PLAN T7.

queue.task: Hunt remaining test failures: run batches of test files, fix what's broken
queue.summary: Hunted remaining failures: ACID compliance concurrent readers (stale result cache after DML) — FIXED. Ran all 656 test files individually with 10s timeout — 0 failures found. Estimated 100% pass rate.

queue.task: Run full regression sweep to verify quality
queue.summary: Already done during T17 — all 656 test files passed individually.

queue.task: Mid-session housekeeping
queue.summary: Git pushed, daily log updated, CURRENT.md updated.

queue.task: Monkey lang: what's the hardest remaining problem? WASM closures, GC, or language feature depth?
queue.summary: Direction: Monkey bytecode compiler + VM (Book 2). Natural next step from tree-walking interpreter. Will implement: bytecode instruction set, stack VM, compiler from AST to bytecode, symbol tables.

queue.goal: Monkey lang improvement: pick one deep feature to implement
queue.summary: Planned 8 BUILD tasks for Monkey bytecode compiler + VM. Steps: bytecode defs → compiler → VM → conditionals → globals → data structures → functions/closures → parity tests.

queue.task: Implement bytecode instruction set (code.js): opcodes, make, lookup, disassemble
queue.summary: Bytecode instruction set (code.js): 30 opcodes, make/readOperands/disassemble/concatInstructions. 12 tests pass.

queue.task: Implement compiler: AST → bytecode. Handle integer literals, infix expressions, booleans
queue.summary: Bytecode compiler (compiler.js): compiles all AST nodes to bytecode. Handles int/string/bool literals, infix/prefix, conditionals, let statements, arrays, hashes, functions, closures. SymbolTable with 5 scopes. 32 tests pass.

queue.task: Implement stack VM: execute bytecodes, operand stack, integer arithmetic + comparisons
queue.summary: Stack VM (vm.js): complete bytecode execution engine. Integer arithmetic, booleans, conditionals, globals/locals, strings, arrays, hashes, functions, closures, recursion (fibonacci), builtins. 89 tests pass.

queue.task: Add conditionals and jumps to compiler + VM (if/else)
queue.summary: Already implemented in T19+T20.

queue.task: Add global bindings: let statements, identifier resolution
queue.summary: Already implemented.

queue.task: Add string, array, hash support in compiler + VM
queue.summary: Already implemented.

queue.task: Add function compilation: OpCall, OpReturn, local bindings, closures
queue.summary: Already implemented.

queue.task: Test parity: verify compiler+VM matches tree-walker for all existing test cases
queue.summary: 84 parity tests: compiler+VM matches tree-walker for all test cases. Fixed top-level return bug. Total: 257 tests pass. Git pushed.

queue.task: End-of-session housekeeping
queue.summary: Deferred - not end of session yet, 5+ hours remain.

queue.task: Monkey VM performance: benchmark compiler+VM vs tree-walker. How much faster is the bytecode path? What are the bottlenecks?
queue.summary: VM is 2.1x faster than tree-walker on fibonacci(30): 1131ms vs 2322ms. Consistent 2x across fib(25) too. Bottleneck: per-opcode switch dispatch + MonkeyInteger allocation. Optimizations possible: direct threading, constant folding, tail calls.

queue.task: Add VM benchmark: fibonacci(35), array processing, string ops. Compare compiler+VM vs tree-walker speed.
queue.summary: VM benchmark suite: 2.5x speedup on fib(25), small programs have VM overhead. Fixed CRITICAL let-binding compilation order bug — value now compiled before symbol definition, fixes counter closure semantics. Also fixed via defineFunctionName for recursive functions. Stack size increased to 8192. All 257 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: What's the next deep architectural improvement? File-backed integration, WAL-based recovery testing, or query plan caching?
queue.summary: File-backed persistence stack exists and is integrated in TransactionalDatabase. Next valuable work: adversarial query stress testing to find more bugs.

queue.goal: HenryDB: adversarial SQL queries — craft intentionally tricky queries that stress edge cases
queue.summary: Found and fixed nested parenthesized expression parser bug. parseComparison had broken paren handler — now defers to parsePrimary. ((1+2)*3) and ((((((1+2)*3)-4)/5)+6)*7) now work. Also identified: self-join column ambiguity, EXISTS subquery, IN with NULL. 656 test files pass.

queue.task: Fix parser: nested parenthesized expressions in parseComparison (DONE)
queue.summary: Already done during T29 PLAN.

queue.task: Write nested-paren and adversarial query test suite
queue.summary: 35 adversarial query tests: nested parens, NULL handling, aggregate edge cases, subqueries, UNION, CTEs, UPDATE/DELETE edge cases, expression ORDER BY, complex boolean logic. All pass.

queue.task: Mid-session housekeeping: daily log, git push, CURRENT.md
queue.summary: Git pushed both repos, daily log updated, CURRENT.md updated. 22 tasks completed.

queue.task: Monkey: add REPL with --engine flag to switch between tree-walker and compiler+VM
queue.summary: REPL with --engine=vm|interpreter|both. VM mode persists globals+symbol table across lines. Both modes produce correct results for closures, recursion, builtins.

queue.task: What's the most interesting edge case in the Monkey compiler? Test closures-over-closures, mutual recursion, tail call patterns.
queue.summary: Tested edge cases: closure chains (✓), closure arrays (✓), compose (✓), tail accumulator sum(100) (✓), array of closures (✓). Found: identifiers cant have digits (Monkey spec). Known limitation: forward reference in closures (Y combinator pattern) doesnt work due to compile-time symbol resolution.

queue.task: Monkey: stress test the compiler+VM with pathological programs (deep recursion, huge arrays, many closures)
queue.summary: 19 stress tests: deep recursion (sum 200, fib 20, ackermann), large arrays (100 elements), many closures, map/filter/reduce combos, fibonacci sequence builder, edge cases. Total: 276 Monkey tests pass.

queue.task: Monkey: What's the next meaningful compiler optimization? Integer caching, tail call elimination, or constant folding?
queue.summary: Integer caching is quick but modest impact. Tail call elimination is the big win — solves stack overflow for recursive patterns, teaches fundamental optimization. Will do both: integer caching first (quick), then TCE.

queue.goal: Monkey: implement integer caching for small integers (-1 to 256) to reduce allocations
queue.summary: Planned: integer cache + tail call optimization.

queue.task: Implement integer cache in VM (pre-allocate -1 to 256)
queue.summary: Integer cache (-1 to 256) pre-allocates MonkeyInteger objects. Reduces GC pressure for small-number arithmetic. 276 tests pass.

queue.task: Add tail call optimization to compiler and VM
queue.summary: Tail call optimization: OpCall+OpReturnValue → OpTailCall. sum(100000,0) works in 71ms with constant stack. Before: stack overflow at 5000. Peephole optimization in compiler + frame reuse in VM. 276 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: What SQL features are most commonly tested in database benchmarks (TPC-H)? What are we missing?
queue.summary: Key missing: NULLS FIRST/LAST, EXISTS correctness, multi-table JOIN testing. TPC-H also needs date arithmetic which we partially support. Priority: fix EXISTS, add NULLS FIRST/LAST, then stress multi-way JOINs.

queue.goal: HenryDB: implement ORDER BY NULLS FIRST/LAST and window function improvements
queue.summary: Planned: NULLS FIRST/LAST + EXISTS fix + tests

queue.task: Implement ORDER BY NULLS FIRST/LAST syntax in parser and evaluator
queue.summary: Fixed NULLS FIRST/LAST parser bug — FIRST was tokenized as KEYWORD not IDENT. All 4 combinations work: ASC/DESC × NULLS FIRST/LAST. No regressions.

queue.task: Fix EXISTS subquery for correlated queries
queue.summary: Fixed correlated EXISTS subquery. Root cause: decorrelate optimizer isCorrelated() conflated table names with aliases. FROM t t2 had innerTables={t,t2} instead of {t2}, making outer ref t.a appear inner. Fix: only add alias to innerTables. Also enhanced _resolveColumn for qualified names. All 656 test files pass.

queue.task: Write tests for NULLS FIRST/LAST and EXISTS
queue.summary: 14 tests for NULLS FIRST/LAST (7) and correlated EXISTS (7). All pass.

queue.task: Monkey: add alphanumeric identifier support to lexer (allow digits in identifiers after first char)
queue.summary: Alphanumeric identifiers: digits allowed after first char. add1, x2, count0 all work. 276 tests pass.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, daily log
queue.summary: Git pushed both repos, daily log updated. Session running 1h20m, 35+ tasks.

queue.task: HenryDB: investigate query plan caching — are we caching prepared statement plans? Test with parameterized queries.
queue.summary: PREPARE/EXECUTE works correctly with parameterized queries. Plan cache and prepared statements functional. Result cache also exists for SELECT queries.

queue.task: HenryDB: implement ORDER BY NULLS FIRST/LAST syntax
queue.summary: Already done in T41.

queue.task: HenryDB: improve EXISTS subquery evaluation (currently returns wrong results for correlated EXISTS)
queue.summary: Already done in T49.

queue.task: End-of-session housekeeping
queue.summary: Deferred — not end of session, 4.5 hours remain.

queue.task: What would make HenryDB stand out? What feature would be genuinely impressive for a from-scratch JS database?
queue.summary: Standout features: TPC-H validation, WASM browser runtime, JSON operators, or EXPLAIN ANALYZE. TPC-H is most impressive for this session — proves end-to-end completeness.

queue.goal: HenryDB: implement a TPC-H micro-benchmark (queries 1, 6, 14 — simple but representative)
queue.summary: Planned 3 tasks: schema+data gen, Q1, Q6+Q14 with benchmarking.

queue.task: Create TPC-H micro schema (lineitem, part tables) with data generator
queue.summary: TPC-H micro-benchmark: Q1 24ms (6 groups, 6 aggregates), Q6 13ms (filter+SUM), Q14 works via 2 queries (arithmetic-between-aggregates limitation). 1000 lineitem, 200 parts. All tests pass.

queue.task: Implement TPC-H Q1 (pricing summary report) and verify correctness
queue.summary: Already done in T53.

queue.task: Implement TPC-H Q6 (revenue forecast) and Q14 (promotion effect) with benchmark timing
queue.summary: Already done in T53.

queue.task: Monkey: add while loops to the language (extends beyond Book 1)
queue.summary: While loops added to Monkey: lexer keyword, parser, AST node, evaluator, compiler (jump-back), VM. Limitation: needs mutation support for practical use. 285 tests pass.

queue.task: Monkey: add string comparison operators (==, !=, <, >) to VM
queue.summary: Fixed string comparisons in VM: value-based instead of reference identity. ==, !=, <, > all work for strings. 285 tests pass.

queue.task: Housekeeping
queue.summary: Housekeeping done.

queue.task: Monkey: what makes this compiler interesting? Document the architecture for the README.
queue.summary: Interesting aspects: dual engines (tree-walker + VM), TCO, full closures, integer cache, 285 tests with parity verification, language extensions (while, alphanumeric ids).

queue.task: Monkey: update README with architecture docs (lexer → parser → compiler → VM pipeline)
queue.summary: Comprehensive README: architecture docs, dual engines, TCO, examples, benchmarks, 285 tests, language extensions.

queue.task: HenryDB: fix arithmetic between aggregates (SUM(a)/SUM(b) should return 1 row, not N)
queue.summary: Fixed arithmetic between aggregates. SUM(a)/SUM(b) now returns 1 row. Added _exprContainsAggregate() for recursive detection and _evalAggregateExpr() for evaluation. TPC-H Q14 now works as single query. 657 test files pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add variable mutation (SET statement) to Monkey for while loop utility
queue.summary: Added set statement for variable mutation. while+set now works: sum(1..10)=55, factorial(5)=120. Both engines. 294 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add EXPLAIN ANALYZE (time each stage, show actual vs estimated rows)
queue.summary: EXPLAIN ANALYZE already implemented! Shows table scan engine, estimated/actual rows, selectivity, planning time, execution time. Estimates are accurate (GROUP BY 3=3 exact, filtered 79=79 exact after ANALYZE).

queue.task: Final housekeeping and daily log
queue.summary: Deferred — 4 hours remain.

queue.task: Monkey: add for loop syntax (for (init; condition; update) { body })
queue.summary: C-style for loops: for (let i=0; i<10; set i=i+1) { body }. Works in both engines. 294 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add JSON_EXTRACT function for querying JSON columns
queue.summary: 7 JSON functions added. JSON_EXTRACT with path navigation. 657 test files pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add JSON_ARRAY_LENGTH and JSON_TYPE functions
queue.summary: Already done in T66.

queue.task: Monkey: update README with set/while/for loop docs and examples
queue.summary: README updated with set/while/for docs, imperative examples, 294 tests.

queue.task: Final session housekeeping
queue.summary: Deferred — 3.5 hours remain.

queue.task: HenryDB: comprehensive JSON function test suite
queue.summary: 27 JSON function tests: extract (9 cases), type, array_length (4), object, array, valid (3), WHERE filtering (3), GROUP BY. All pass.

queue.task: Monkey: add modulo operator (%) to lexer, parser, evaluator, and VM
queue.summary: Modulo operator (%) added to all layers. OpMod opcode. 10%3=1. 294 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add -> and ->> operators for JSON field access (PostgreSQL-style)
queue.summary: Deferred — requires tokenizer changes, risky for regressions. JSON_EXTRACT covers the use case.

queue.task: Final housekeeping and daily log
queue.summary: Deferred — 4 hours remain.

queue.task: Monkey: FizzBuzz showcase — write a complete FizzBuzz using for/set/% that works in both engines
queue.summary: FizzBuzz showcase: for/set/% working in both engines. 4 tests including parity and FizzBuzz(100).

queue.task: HenryDB: add LATERAL JOIN support for correlated lateral subqueries
queue.summary: Deferred — LATERAL JOIN is complex, multi-table JOIN tests are higher priority.

queue.task: HenryDB: write comprehensive multi-table JOIN stress tests (3-4 way joins)
queue.summary: 9 multi-table JOIN tests: 3-way and 4-way joins, revenue calc, HAVING, WHERE+GROUP BY, LEFT JOIN chain, self-join, subquery with JOIN. All pass.

queue.task: Monkey: add string indexing (s[0]) and string slicing builtins (slice, charAt)
queue.summary: String indexing (hello[0]=h) + type/str/int builtins. Both engines. 298 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add GENERATE_SERIES table function for range generation
queue.summary: GENERATE_SERIES already implemented! 1..5, step 2, SUM(1..100)=5050 all work.

queue.task: Housekeeping: daily log, git push, CURRENT.md
queue.summary: Housekeeping: daily log, CURRENT.md, git push. 55+ tasks complete.

queue.task: HenryDB: implement MERGE statement (UPSERT) - INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE
queue.summary: UPSERT (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) already implemented and working.

queue.task: Monkey: implement string interpolation or format function
queue.summary: format() builtin: format("%s is %d", "Alice", 30). Both engines. 298 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add RETURNING clause for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
queue.summary: RETURNING clause already implemented and working for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

queue.task: HenryDB: write comprehensive test for JSON + GROUP BY + HAVING + ORDER BY combined
queue.summary: 9 combined JSON+SQL tests: GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY, AVG, subquery, CTE, CASE with JSON. All pass.

queue.task: Final session housekeeping
queue.summary: Deferred — 3.5 hours remain.

queue.task: Monkey: implement constant folding optimization pass (evaluate 2+3 at compile time)
queue.summary: Constant folding: 2+3→5, (2+3)*4-1→19, string concat folded. Recursive through nested exprs. Variables unfolded. 298 tests.

queue.task: HenryDB: implement INTERSECT and EXCEPT set operations
queue.summary: INTERSECT and EXCEPT already implemented. Both work correctly.

queue.task: HenryDB: add comprehensive window function tests (RANK, ROW_NUMBER, NTILE)
queue.summary: 11 comprehensive window function tests: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, SUM/AVG/COUNT OVER, partitioned, multiple windows, with WHERE. All pass.

queue.task: Final housekeeping
queue.summary: Deferred — 3.5 hours remain.

queue.task: Monkey: dead code elimination (remove unreachable code after return)
queue.summary: Dead code elimination: statements after return are removed. fn(){return 1; 2; 3;} → only emits return 1. 298 tests.

queue.task: HenryDB: add COALESCE, NULLIF, GREATEST, LEAST functions and test them
queue.summary: COALESCE, NULLIF, GREATEST, LEAST all already implemented and working.

queue.task: Monkey: add range builtin for creating arrays (range(1, 10) → [1,2,...,9])
queue.summary: range() builtin: range(5)→[0..4], range(1,5)→[1..4], step support, negative step. Both engines. 298 tests.

queue.task: Final session housekeeping
queue.summary: Deferred — continuing work.

queue.task: HenryDB: write a real-world showcase test — e-commerce data warehouse with 5 tables, complex queries, JSON, CTEs, windows
queue.summary: 11 e-commerce showcase tests: 5-table schema, 4-way JOINs, CLV ranking, JSON analysis, CTE trends, NOT EXISTS anti-join, window partitions, CASE basket analysis. Found PK+EXISTS bug (tracked). All pass.

queue.task: Monkey: comprehensive builtin test suite (all builtins: len, first, last, rest, push, puts, type, str, int, format, range)
queue.summary: 32 builtin tests with parity (both engines). All 11 builtins covered. 330 total Monkey tests pass.

queue.task: Final housekeeping
HenryDB: fix PK index scan + correlated EXISTS bug (tracked from showcase test)
queue.summary: Fixed result cache caching correlated subquery results. Cache now skips when _outerRow is set. PK+EXISTS works. Showcase test restored with PRIMARY KEYs. All tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: update README with current feature list and test count
queue.summary: HenryDB README updated: full feature list, 666 test files, TPC-H benchmarks, architecture, example queries.

queue.task: Final session housekeeping
